moderatedChannels.title: Online channels you moderate
moderatedChannels.reconnect: Reconnect Twitch to refresh your moderated channel access.
moderatedChannels.empty: No moderated channels are live right now.

enableBot.title: Enable the bot
enableBot.body: Turn on bot control in Settings before using requests on your channel.

offlineTesting.title: Offline testing is on
offlineTesting.body: Requests stay available while you test the bot offline.

goLive.title: Go live to start taking requests
goLive.body: Requests are meant to run while your channel is live.

requestsPaused.title: Requests are paused
requestsPaused.body: Re-enable requests in Settings when you want viewers to add songs again.

channelReady.title: Channel is ready
channelReady.body: Your stream is live and requests are available.

checkStatus.title: Check channel status
checkStatus.body: Make sure the bot is connected before you start taking requests.

header.title: Settings
header.description: Owner-only channel configuration for requests, moderators, and the stream overlay.
header.betaNote: Beta testers can install the Twitch extension panel on Twitch.

states.saveFailed: Save failed. Review the message below and try again.

tabs.caption: Categories
tabs.helper: Switch categories without losing changes. Save once when you are ready.

general.label: General
general.description: Core channel behavior, playlist defaults, and chat command settings.

access.label: Access
access.description: Choose who can request songs from your viewers.

vipCosts.label: Duration limits
vipCosts.description: Set how longer songs change VIP token pricing.

filters.label: Filters
filters.description: Limit search results and control which songs can be requested.

limits.label: Limits
limits.description: Control playlist size and request pacing.

rewards.label: Granting VIP tokens
rewards.description: Configure automatic VIP token rewards and cooldowns.

requestModifiers.label: Request modifiers
requestModifiers.description: Let viewers request specific playable parts and decide whether modifiers spend VIP tokens.

moderation.label: Moderation
moderation.description: Review channel instructions, rule enforcement, and moderator controls.

overlay.label: Overlay
overlay.description: Tune the stream overlay without leaving the page.

ownerOnly.title: Owner settings only
ownerOnly.description: This area is only available for channels you own. Use the channel page for moderation work on channels you manage.
ownerOnly.signInHint: Sign in with a streamer account that owns a channel to manage owner settings here.

channelSetup.title: Channel setup
channelSetup.description: Control the main playlist toggles, chat command, and viewer-facing playlist behavior.
channelSetup.reconnectNotice: Twitch permissions need to be refreshed before the bot can run.
channelSetup.enableBotHelp: Turn this off when you do not want RockList.Live actions, including awarding VIP tokens for subscriptions, raids, etc.
channelSetup.enableRequestsHelp: Turning requests off keeps playlist management available to you and your moderators, but viewers cannot add songs.
channelSetup.chatDefaultsHelp: Choose the default language and command behavior for viewers.
channelSetup.showPickOrderBadgesHelpAfter: so you can see which request is each viewer's next pick after a shuffle.
channelSetup.commandPrefixHelp: Use the command viewers type in chat.
channelSetup.requestModifiersHelp: Let viewers request a specific playable part when a song includes one.
channelSetup.requestModifiersPreamble: Use this if you want viewers to be able to request a path. For example, if you usually play guitar, you can let viewers request bass or a specific guitar part.
channelSetup.allowedPathModifiersHelp: Enable one or more modifiers. Viewers can only choose enabled options when the song includes a matching part.
channelSetup.modifierBehaviorHelp: Choose whether paid modifiers stay in the regular queue or use VIP priority. The modifier cost is the total extra cost.
channelSetup.pathModifierVipTokenCost: VIP token cost when a modifier is used
channelSetup.pathModifierVipTokenCostHelp: Set to 0 to let viewers use a modifier without VIP tokens.
channelSetup.pathModifierUsesVipPriority: Paid modifiers also use VIP priority
channelSetup.pathModifierUsesVipPriorityHelp: The modifier cost is the total extra cost.
channelSetup.duplicateCooldownHelp: Set how long the same song waits before it can be requested again.

requestAccess.title: Who can request
requestAccess.description: Choose which viewers can add songs to your playlist.

filters.title: Search and request filters
filters.description: These rules limit what appears in search and what viewers can request on your channel page.

officialDlc.title: Official DLC
officialDlc.description: Limit search results and requests to official DLC.
officialDlc.notice: Official DLC filters are not available yet. This section previews settings that appear here in a future update.
officialDlc.onlyOwned: Only include official DLC that I own

allowedTunings.title: Allowed tunings
allowedTunings.description: Click any tuning to allow or block it.

requiredPaths.title: Required paths
requiredPaths.description: Choose the paths a song needs before it appears in search or can be requested.
requiredPaths.none: No path filter is active. Songs can match with any path combination.
requiredPaths.singleSuffix: They can still include any other paths.
requiredPaths.nonMatchExampleLabel: Will not match: a song with
requiredPaths.singleNonMatchExample: does not match because it does not include the selected path.
requiredPaths.matchAnyPrefix: Songs match if they include at least one of
requiredPaths.matchAnySuffix: They can still include any other paths.
requiredPaths.matchAnyAllSelectedSuffix: Songs can match with any path combination.
requiredPaths.matchAnyExample: still matches because it includes one selected path.
requiredPaths.matchAnyNonMatchExample: does not match because it does not include any selected path.
requiredPaths.matchAllPrefix: Songs match only if they include all of
requiredPaths.matchAllSuffix: They can still include any other paths.
requiredPaths.matchAllExample: still matches because it includes every selected path.
requiredPaths.matchAllNonMatchExample: does not match because it is missing one selected path.

queueLimits.title: Queue and rate limits
queueLimits.description: Set the playlist size and decide how often regular or VIP requests can be added.

vipAutomation.title: VIP token automation
vipAutomation.description: Automatically reward VIP tokens for new subs, gifted subs, cheers, channel point rewards, raids, and StreamElements tips.
vipAutomation.newSub: Give 1 VIP token for a new paid sub
vipAutomation.sharedRenewal: Give 1 VIP token for a shared sub renewal message
vipAutomation.sharedRenewalHelp: Sub renewals will only grant a VIP token if it is shared in chat.
vipAutomation.subGifter: Give 1 VIP token to the gifter for each gifted sub
vipAutomation.subRecipient: Give 1 VIP token to each gifted sub recipient
vipAutomation.channelPointsToggle: Give 1 VIP token for a channel point reward
vipAutomation.channelPointCostHelp: Each redemption of this reward gives 1 VIP token.
vipAutomation.channelPointRewardNameHelp: RockList.Live creates and keeps this Twitch reward in sync for you.
vipAutomation.channelPointSetupHelp: RockList.Live creates this reward after you save settings. Viewers redeem it on Twitch when your channel is live.
vipAutomation.channelPointSetupNote: This requires a Twitch Affiliate or Partner channel and works when the bot is enabled for your channel. If Twitch asks you to reconnect, reconnect your Twitch account and save again.
vipAutomation.channelPointUnavailable: This Twitch channel is not Affiliate or Partner right now, so RockList.Live cannot create a channel point reward here yet.

channelPointInstructions.title: Channel point reward setup
channelPointInstructions.description: Turn this on here, reconnect Twitch if prompted, and let RockList.Live create and manage the reward for you. This requires a Twitch Affiliate or Partner channel.
channelPointInstructions.rewardNameHelp: This is the exact Twitch reward name RockList.Live creates.

enable.title: Turn on the reward here
enable.body: Start in this dashboard so RockList.Live knows the point cost and can create the reward.

steps.toggle: Enable Give 1 VIP token for a channel point reward.
steps.cost: Set how many channel points each redemption should cost.
steps.save: Save settings so RockList.Live can create or update the reward.

reconnect.title: Reconnect Twitch if needed
reconnect.body: Some older Twitch connections are missing the reward-management scope.

steps.notice: If you see a reconnect notice on this page, click Reconnect Twitch.
steps.signIn: Finish that Twitch sign-in with the broadcaster account that owns this channel.
steps.saveAgain: Return here and save again so RockList.Live can finish creating the reward.

check.title: Confirm it on Twitch
check.body: Once the reward is created, viewers can redeem it directly from Twitch.

steps.reward: Look for the reward name shown below in your Twitch channel point rewards.
steps.live: When your channel is live, viewers can redeem it to earn 1 VIP token.
steps.fulfill: RockList.Live fulfills the redemption and adds the VIP token automatically.

vipAutomation.raidReward: Give 1 VIP token to the streamer who raids this channel
vipAutomation.raidNotice: Twitch only sends raid rewards here when the raid shows up in chat.
vipAutomation.minimumCheer: Minimum cheer to earn a partial token
vipAutomation.minimumCheerExample: Minimum cheer: {bits} bits grants {tokenCount} of a VIP token at the {percent}% threshold.
vipAutomation.bitsExample: {oneTokenBits} bits grants 1 VIP token. {fiveTokenBits} bits grants 5 VIP tokens.
vipAutomation.bitsExampleEmpty: Set the bits per VIP token above 0 to preview the minimum cheer threshold.
vipAutomation.tipsToggle: Give VIP tokens for StreamElements tips
vipAutomation.tipAmountHelp: A $25 tip grants 5 VIP tokens when this is set to 5.
vipAutomation.relayUrlHelp: Use this URL in the Streamer.bot step that forwards your StreamElements tip event.
vipAutomation.setupHelp: StreamElements can keep showing tip alerts in OBS the way you already use them. VIP token rewards need Streamer.bot to forward the tip event here.
vipAutomation.setupNote: Tip rewards only work when the StreamElements tip name matches the viewer's Twitch username.
vipAutomation.songLengthHelp: Require more VIP tokens for longer songs when the song duration is known.
vipAutomation.songLengthExample: Songs over {minutes} minutes require {count} VIP tokens.
vipAutomation.songLengthEmpty: No song-length VIP thresholds are active yet.
vipAutomation.songLengthNote: If a song does not have a known duration, viewers can still add it normally and you can handle VIP tokens manually.
vipAutomation.vipCooldownHelp: After a viewer adds a VIP request, make them wait before adding another one. Regular requests still follow your normal rules.
vipAutomation.vipCooldownToggle: Require a cooldown before a viewer can add another VIP request
vipAutomation.vipCooldownExample: After a VIP request is added, that viewer must wait {minutes} minutes before adding another VIP request.
vipAutomation.vipCooldownNote: If the viewer removes that VIP request before it is played, the cooldown ends and they can add another VIP request right away.

instructions.title: StreamElements tip relay setup
instructions.description: Use Streamer.bot to forward each StreamElements tip here so RockList.Live can award VIP tokens automatically.

enable.title: Turn on tip rewards here
enable.body: Start in this dashboard so the relay has somewhere to send the tip.

steps.toggle: Enable Give VIP tokens for StreamElements tips.
steps.amount: Set how much tip value awards 1 VIP token.
steps.save: Save settings before you run your first test tip.

connect.title: Connect Streamer.bot to StreamElements
connect.body: Set up Streamer.bot to receive StreamElements tip events.

steps.connect: Click Connect to StreamElements and sign in with the StreamElements account that receives your tips.
steps.autoConnect: Turn on Auto Connect so tip relays keep working when Streamer.bot restarts.
steps.action: Create a new Action and add the StreamElements Tip trigger to it.

relay.title: Add the relay step
relay.body: In that same Action, add an Execute C# Code sub-action. Use the webhook URL and paste-in code below.

steps.subAction: Add an Execute C# Code sub-action inside the Action that uses the StreamElements Tip trigger.
steps.paste: Paste the webhook URL and the code below, then save the Action.

relay.webhookHelp: Paste this exact URL into the Streamer.bot code below.
relay.codeHelp: Paste this into the Execute C# Code sub-action.

test.title: Send a test tip
test.body: Run one test tip all the way through before you depend on it live.

steps.tip: Send a test tip through StreamElements after the Action is enabled.
steps.chat: Watch for the RockList.Live chat reply that confirms VIP tokens were added.
steps.username: Make sure the StreamElements tip name is the viewer's Twitch login. If it is not, RockList.Live cannot match the tip to that viewer.
steps.obs: Without Streamer.bot forwarding the event, StreamElements alerts can still show in OBS, but no VIP tokens are awarded here.

setupSteps.trigger: Use the StreamElements Tip trigger in Streamer.bot.
setupSteps.send: Send that tip event to the Relay URL shown here.
setupSteps.note: Without Streamer.bot, tips still show in OBS, but they do not add VIP tokens here.

channelInstructions.title: Copy and paste summary
channelInstructions.description: Copy a ready-to-paste summary for your channel page.
channelInstructions.requestSong: Use {requestCommand} artist - song to request a song.
channelInstructions.requestRandom: Use {requestCommand} artist *random for a random match.
channelInstructions.requestChoice: Use {requestCommand} artist *choice for a streamer choice request.
channelInstructions.editCurrent: Use {editCommand} artist - song to change your current request.
channelInstructions.editPosition: Use {editCommand} #2 artist - song to edit a specific queued request.
channelInstructions.vipRequest: Use {vipCommand} artist - song to make a request VIP and move it to the top.
channelInstructions.vipBalance: Use {vipCommand} on its own to check your VIP token balance.
channelInstructions.vipBaseCost: VIP requests add {countText} and play next.
channelInstructions.pathTitle: How to choose a specific part
channelInstructions.pathFree: Add {modifiers} to {requestCommand}, {vipCommand}, or {editCommand} when the song includes a matching part.
channelInstructions.pathPaidVip: Add {modifiers} to {vipCommand} artist - song when the song includes a matching part. Choosing a part adds {countText} and uses VIP priority.
channelInstructions.pathPaidRegular: Add {modifiers} to {requestCommand}, {vipCommand}, or {editCommand} when the song includes a matching part. Choosing a part adds {countText}.
channelInstructions.pathPaidRegularNote: Use {vipCommand} as well to play next. VIP adds 1 more VIP token.
channelInstructions.otherPosition: Use {positionCommand} to check your place in the playlist.
channelInstructions.otherRemove: Use {removeCommand} reg, {removeCommand} vip, or {removeCommand} all to remove your requests.
channelInstructions.rewardsNone: VIP tokens are not awarded automatically right now.
channelInstructions.rewardChannelPoints: Channel point reward: 1 VIP token per redemption ({cost} points).
channelInstructions.rewardRaid: Raids: 1 VIP token for raids of {count}+ viewers.

rules.title: Blacklist and setlist rules
rules.description: Blacklist and setlist entries are managed on the channel page. These toggles control how those rules are enforced.

moderatorPermissions.title: Moderator permissions
moderatorPermissions.description: Moderators always see VIP tokens. Turn other channel-management actions on or off here.

overlay.title: Stream overlay
overlay.description: Display your playlist on your stream using a browser source.

states.failedToSave: Overlay settings could not be saved.

theme.title: Theme
theme.backgroundColorHelp: For a transparent background, set background opacity to 0.

restoreDialog.title: Restore defaults?
restoreDialog.description: This resets the overlay editor to the default theme. Unsaved changes will be lost.
